hotel_name: The Roosevelt New Orleans, A Waldorf Astoria Hotel

reviews.title: Everything is 5 Star!
reviews.text: Perfectly located a few blocks outside of the excitement, noise, and fun on Bourbon Street,  this is a grand old hotel that is clean, quite, and provides outstanding service.  It is close to all attractions whether driving or walking.

reviews.title: Will stay there every time I’m in New Orleans, great hotel!
reviews.positives: Hotel was clean, staff was friendly, very comfortable stay!

reviews.title: Bourbon Street adjacent
reviews.text: Beautiful location, near everything in the Bourbon District. Old charm.

reviews.title: Loud banging ac
reviews.positives: Landmark hotel
reviews.negatives: AC was awful. Loud bang as it started and stopped. Couldn’t sleep all night. Front desk told us we just had bad luck with the room they gave us . Said lots of the rooms are like that. No compensation was made for us.

reviews.title: Five Star Hotel with One Star Service
reviews.text: This is a five star hotel with 1 star service. It's a gorgeous, grand hotel and our suite could not have been more spacious or nicer. However, service was AWFUL. Each time I tried calling for small items--like a missing "privacy" sign and missing slippers--there was no answer, or only an answer on the third try after 20 rings. And, it took at least 45-60 minutes each time to get what I needed, except when it didn't come at all. I finally had to go downstairs to get a manager. Fortunately, he gave me his cell number so I could call him the NEXT time the front desk/personal assistant didn't answer. Other small annoyances were things like stacks of dirty towels all around the pool area, with no pool attendant visible. This is branded as a Waldorf Astoria hotel and is an embarrassment to the brand.

reviews.title: Beautiful rooms, historical interior, fun bar. Classy and fun.
reviews.positives: Historical and central.

reviews.title: Lovely Romantic Stay
reviews.text: Stayed for a quick weekend trip. Grand lobby, pool with attendants and wait service, lovely touches and amenities in room, comfy beds. The location is right off of canal so fairly central. I would absolutely book here again.

reviews.title: Pleasant
reviews.positives: Location for Touring
reviews.negatives: Room a little small

reviews.title: Few issues
reviews.text: Noisy Air Cleaner keep us from sleeping.

reviews.title: Perfect place for our honeymoon.
reviews.positives: It’s absolutely a beautiful Hotel.
reviews.negatives: They treat you like royalty and everyone is so nice.

reviews.title: A Great Hotel Experience
reviews.text: The room was spacious, and the bed was very comfortable.

reviews.title: It was fabulous
reviews.positives: Everything
reviews.negatives: Nothing

reviews.title: Fabulous stay
reviews.text: The hotel is gorgeous, spacious rooms, beds super comfortable & just 2 blocks from Bourbon. Well worth the money in my opinion.

reviews.title: Fantastic place close to CBD …. Bourbon St
reviews.positives: Friendly staff from the moment you pull up at the front
reviews.negatives: Nothing to complain about

reviews.title: Charming Hotel
reviews.text: Beautiful hotel in a great location, friendly staff, very clean, beautiful pool.

reviews.title: Not worth what I paid, great to visit but not to stay.
reviews.positives: It a beautiful hotel in a nice location, most of the staff are very helpful and friendly. For an older hotel it’s in good shape. Two of the bartenders were excellent
reviews.negatives: Beds are very uncomfortable, nothing feels luxurious as a guest, shower was broken, requests were not able to be accommodated, housekeeping missed turndowns, some staff were not helpful and gave conflicting information

reviews.title: Expensive but worth it
reviews.text: Experience was near perfect.  Only complaint, two champagne glasses were left in the hall for 48 hours.  Of course, this is New Orleans.

reviews.title: Overall, great experience and was as expected
reviews.positives: The hotel did well to accommodate the wedding party ahead of our check-in and took care of our bags. Allowed us to browse the French Quarter and have a New Orleans famous Muffuletta from Central Grocery & Deli before checking into the room. Staff were all very friendly and helpful throughout! Also very good breakfast buffet as well each morning.
reviews.negatives: One challenge was parking, but having the public parking garage across the street was convenient as no one where to park on the road safely. We reserved a King Suite and the room and lounge was great, however, did find the bathroom a bit too small for this category of room.

reviews.title: Great location!
reviews.text: Great location, able to walk to all the main tourist destinations. The service was amazing and rooms were clean.

reviews.title: Great property and service
reviews.positives: Nighttime room fix up with chocolate
reviews.negatives: Like being in quarter better

reviews.title: Only problem is that the internet is so slow
reviews.positives: Comfort, location, and service

reviews.title: Perfectly located near the Sanger Theatre!
reviews.positives: Fantastic location within a block of the Sanger Theatre. We visited New Orleans specifically to see the Phantom of the opera and stayed at the Roosevelt hotel during our visit. Everything was fantastic, including the staff and the restaurants.

reviews.title: Beautiful hotel, Great location
reviews.text: We had a nice stay at the hotel.  It is less than two blocks from Bourbon Street and the surrounding French Quarter.  The hotel lobby is beautiful and the room was very pleasant.  Our room was not yet ready by the time we had arrived and we were each given a $12 drink voucher at their very nice bar.  We ordered $15 drinks with our vouchers and were not charged for the remaining $6.
reviews.positives: Location, Beautiful Lobby, Nice bar

reviews.title: I was totally with everything
reviews.positives: Location
reviews.negatives: No problems

reviews.title: genial!
reviews.text: excellence hotel con professional es que hacen que tu estancia sea maravillosa
reviews.positives: ubicacion , servico, trato

reviews.title: Beautiful historic hotel in a central location for your visit in New Orleans. We’d love to come back again!
reviews.positives: The Roosevelt’s excellent location, amenities, staff, history and beautiful structure are a standout.  With our having a longer stay, it was a plus that they offer several larger room types with the bedroom separate from the living area. Makes for a more comfortable stay.

reviews.title: bel hotel mais peu calme
reviews.text: Beaucoup de monde debout à l'intérieur de la piscine , avec boisson à la main. Peu hygiénique et bruyant. Impossible de nager dans la piscine durant le week-end...la salle pour prendre petit déjeuner rapide sombre et peu de choix.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: The property was nice. The rooms were nice but not special for the price.  The coffee bar was super in the room. The decor and quality of bathroom fixtures weren’t special.

reviews.title: One word… Amazing!
reviews.text:
Love this hotel! 
It has everything you need for a really pleasent stay.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: The old time feel, dedication to customer service.
reviews.negatives: Nothing

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: Service and location were stellar. The aesthetic had such a cozy vibe to it and made my Mardi Gras extremely special. If I'm coming back, I'm doing my best to stay here again. Don't forget about the rooftop lounge either; it's quite nice.
reviews.negatives: Nothing.

reviews.title: Exceeded expectations
reviews.positives: The beauty of the facility, and the cleanliness of the premises and room
reviews.negatives: The location tends to promote unsavory run in interactions with homeless individuals and street beggars almost immediately after departing the property.

reviews.title: Lovely place to get engaged!
reviews.positives:
Attentive staff and lovely room

The decorated lobby was spectacular
reviews.negatives: Nothing

reviews.title: Excellent place, but sleep quality and TV options were awful.
reviews.positives: My wife and I love the building. The decorations, the staff, the location and on site options for eating.
reviews.negatives: My room was hot, had to sit in the chair in front of the window to get some cool air flow, the TV had nothing to watch and couldn't stream from phone or sign into personal Netflix. We watched movies on my tablet until we could fall asleep. Lots of very nice hotels all around for 100s of dollars less. We've stayed at the roosevelt the last 2 times we visited the city. We'll try the sonesta next time.

reviews.title: Overrated
reviews.positives: The hotel was nice and so was the location
reviews.negatives: It was too crowded and crowd control and security was terrible. I realize it's the holidays but esp with such a large crowd there should have been someone at the hotel elevators and also at the back doors monitoring who was going in and out. It is rated a five star hotel but shouldn't be. The Christmas decorations in the lobby are pretty but next time I'd just go there to see them and not stay there (like everyone else).  I couldn't get in the bar or the coffee cafe or even thru the lobby. Hotel guests should have some sort of priority when paying to stay there. Not a good experience at all!

reviews.title: Everything stands out but I can assure you I’ll be back!
reviews.positives: Was some superb
reviews.negatives: Nothing

reviews.title: Stay was adequate but not up to par with a luxury experience.
reviews.positives: Some of the staff (mostly breakfast and restaurant staff, as well as Randy outside were incredibly professional and warm. The architecture of the building is beautiful.
reviews.negatives: The check in and check out staff were completely disinterested. No warm welcome coming in and no questions if we enjoyed our stay as we checked out. Also, the lobby was always incredibly crowded with outside bar hoppers m. It’s not a luxury experience to be fighting my way through boozed up people trying to get to the elevator. Room was tiny- shower too small.

reviews.title: Skip Resturant Dominica
reviews.positives:
I loved the elaborate and beautiful Christmas decoration!
The lobby was a wonderland 
Skip the restaurant, Dominica.
It was the worst food I have ever eaten.
reviews.negatives: The restaurant Dominica

reviews.title: My GOTO Place in NOLA
reviews.positives: First time. Loved it. Great’0ld NOLA’ charm. Very attentive staff.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: all the USB electronic outlets and maglite charging for the phone & coffee station
reviews.negatives: The noise from Canal Street

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: The lobby is spectacular and the suite we were in was spacious and well appointed
reviews.negatives: The bathrooms in the regular rooms are small and have limited counter space

reviews.title: Incredible experience. Amazing staff. Can't wait to return!
reviews.positives: This was the best hotel experience I've had. I knew it was a stellar hotel but was still blown away by how incredible the staff was. They were quick to spot and respond to any needs. Exceptionally kind and gracious.

reviews.title: Everything you needed was just right there.. fun, excitement, accessibility’s
reviews.positives: Kept very clean inside and out and Mr John was my favorite but the staff was willing and ready on any given day.
reviews.negatives: Nothing you can do about certain thing.

reviews.title: Enjoyed my third visit to this great city even more because I stayed at the Roosevelt.
reviews.positives: The style is very nice. The bar and restaurant are great.
reviews.negatives: The vagrants, but know that it's out of the hotels control.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: Spacious, spotless, and elevated decor, good location and staff
reviews.negatives: Nothing

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: Everything about the hotel was so beautiful! It was in walking distance to shopping, food, and entertainment.
reviews.negatives: Don't have anything I didn't like.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: Everything about the Hotel and the staff were excellent!
reviews.negatives: It gets pretty loud in rooms facing Canal Street even on the 10th floor only because Canal Street is very loud.

reviews.title: Best hotels in New Orleans with excellent staff!
reviews.positives: Beautiful historic hotel
reviews.negatives: Nothing

reviews.title: The stay was overall wonderful. I prefer a room facing the street.
reviews.positives: It was classy, elegant, and upscale. Close to everything.
reviews.negatives: I didn’t like the view. I like rooms facing the street.

reviews.title: OK stay in a hotel that needs a renovation and better service.
reviews.positives: The bed was comfortable, location was ok.
reviews.negatives:
Our room on the 6th floor really needs a renovation. The furniture was old, the night stand was positioned 2-3 feet from the back wall so you could (barely) access the extra outlet for your phone. There were 2 outlets near the desk but there were 2 of us and we needed more outlets. Obviously this was a problem for others as the poorly positioned night stand indicated. The bathroom is old and again, needs renovation - after a shower you have to prop the door open (as it closes immediately when released) in order to get some of the steam out and mirrors useable. I ordered breakfast one morning - a yogurt bowl - only to see everyone else served. Finally got there attention and they brought me the wrong order - unfortunately I didn't have to time for them to once again place another order so I ordered a toasted english muffin to go. When I opened the box to eat it - it was raw - not toasted, no butter, no spreads - bad service.
One night we went to the bar after a dinner event and it was full - no place to "perch" and no place to get a drink unless you wanted to take it up to your room. The dining room bar was closed.

reviews.title: We had an amazing visit
reviews.positives: Location.   Everything actually
reviews.negatives: Nothing

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: Everything was amazing
reviews.negatives: Nothing

reviews.title: It was a short stay but we loved the historical beauty as well as the convenience of the amenities.
reviews.positives: It is a beautiful historic hotel located minutes from the theatre where we attended a musical.  We dined at the on site restaurant and the café.

reviews.title: Good enough
reviews.positives: Location, age.
reviews.negatives: Pillows.

reviews.title: Beautiful, historic hotel. Great service and great location.
reviews.positives: The hotel is beautiful and walking distance to the french quarter and trolleys. The rooms were clean and comfortable, excellent in-room dining service. The staff in general was outstanding and professional. We ate at Domenica which was also great. We will definitely be back!
reviews.negatives: The Sazerac bar had great service but is really really crowded on the weekends. It was not a problem, but I added this just because they asked what we didn't like!

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives: Great location, staff very helpful with recommendations.
reviews.negatives: Elevator sides during our stay

reviews.title: Lovely Christmas in New Orleans stay.
reviews.positives: Room was spacious and very quiet. The bed was super comfortable. All very clean. The staff was extremely accommodating. The hotel was the place to be at Christmas for visitors and locals alike, with the entire block-long first floor decorated in Christmas trees , lights, tinsel, the works. Great latte/coffee to go and a varied breakfast/lunch menu in the large cafe. Domenica restaurant is a local foodie favorite and worth a visit. Terrific location across the street from the theater where The Nutcracker ballet was performed.
reviews.negatives: The safe was located in a corner of the closet that was hard to get to. The bathroom walk-in shower was on the small side. When the fold-out sofa was moved, we noticed a stain on the carpet, which was off-putting.

reviews.title: Short, expensive,memorable experience
reviews.positives: I loved the space and the vibe of the overall hotel.
reviews.negatives: I didn’t like the surprise security/insurance deposit I had to put down on the spot. I was in communication with someone at the hotel and that aspect was never mentioned.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives: Location and decoration
reviews.negatives: Crowds in lobby and found the property pricey

reviews.title: Overall fine other than they couldn't care less how long people wait in line to check in.
reviews.positives: Good location, beautiful decorations for Christmas, friendly staff.
reviews.negatives: Check-in process is an utter nightmare. Long lines pretty much all the time we were there. Like waiting for a popular ride at Disneyland.  On top of that, hotel couldn't locate my reservation and we fiddled around for more than an hour before they finally found it. Ugh.

reviews.title: Our room was not ready until 7:30 pm

reviews.title: Perfect four nights for our family.
reviews.positives: Great service. Good food, fast room service, friendly staff. Clean and wonderful. We could not have asked for a better stay for our family of four.
reviews.negatives: Large suite, great service. Excellent location.

reviews.title: Overall it was amazing stay and would stay there again if I visit again.

reviews.title: quick family trip to experience historic new orleans on the way back home from a summer beach vacation made all the more
reviews.positives: historic quality, attentive staff, great cocktail lounge and food
reviews.negatives: It is my favorite stay in New Orleans to date, pricey but nothing bad to say

reviews.title: Always exceeds expectations
reviews.positives: Staff is great. This is an historic place.

reviews.title: Everything was perfect and we will be back!
reviews.positives: The staff was absolutely amazing.  Everyone treated us better than I have ever had from a hotel.  The food in the restaurants were perfect even if you order room service the food still tastes as if you were in the restaurant eating.  I could not have asked for a better staff
reviews.negatives: Nothing.  Everything was perfect

reviews.title: A great time with improvements in the future
reviews.positives: Luxury and location
reviews.negatives:
1. Showers didn’t work properly. 
2. There was an extra $900 hold that nobody told me about it.

reviews.title: Overall a wonderful stay and will do it again in Nov
reviews.positives: The hotel is amazing. Staff was great.
reviews.negatives: There was a dirty Qtip in the hall close to my room. After a day and a half I threw it away myself. Maids walked past several times. Fire alarm kept going off and woke me several times. Pool was nasty and overcrowded. I think they sell memberships.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives: Clean and beautiful
reviews.negatives: Everything was great!

reviews.title: Nice atmosphere
reviews.positives: Beds were very comfy
reviews.negatives: When we arrived , we were given given our room cards and when we went to the room , it was unmade. Towels in floor beds all unmade and we checked in after 4:00. The receptionist was very kind and put us in another room but I don’t think it was what I had originally booked. Never expected to walk into a room well after check in time and it be unmade especially at a 4 star hotel. All in all we had a nice visit

reviews.title: Wonderful and close to the major events including BOURBON Street
reviews.positives: Luxurious and quiet when staying in the hotel however when out and about it was very central located in the heart of NOLA and I enjoyed my espresso (in the room) very much each and every day.
reviews.negatives: Disappointed we could not get a private guide during the time of our stay as the hotel guide was busy and the internet was down in the hotel for a few days

reviews.title: Just ok!
reviews.positives: Location!
reviews.negatives: Cold showers! Housekeeping woke me up at 8:00every morning!

reviews.title: Hotel was beautiful!
reviews.positives: The room was really nice and the hotel itself was beautiful.
reviews.negatives:
The service at the pool was really slow.  

The hostess in the restaurant acted like she was inconvenienced to wait on us.  Very slow with poor communication skills.  Almost rude.

Check-in at the front desk was exceptional!  Very pleasant and professional.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: Great location.. excellent atmosphere
reviews.negatives: Only thing was how small the fridge is.. being that New Orleans is the foodie capital of course you bring leftovers back to the room and I found out the first night that the leftovers will not fit in the fridge but besides that everything was great

reviews.title: Great staff, comfortable rooms, and a fantastic Sazerac to cap off the day.
reviews.positives: Spic and span. Good location. Great staff - very helpful and helped us fix an issue with the fixtures in the room quickly!
reviews.negatives: Ultra-cold lobby. Could be 1-2 degrees warmer :)

reviews.title: Good Stay at The Roosevelt !
reviews.positives: The service from the entire staff ( doorman/ waitress, waiter/cleaning crew and front desk) was EXCELLENT. Their attention to customer service was impeccable. Both my wife and I felt very comfortable and secure at this hotel.
reviews.negatives: N/A

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: The staff was very helpful

reviews.title: Very noisy
reviews.positives: Luxury feel
reviews.negatives: Super Noisy…. It was difficult to get some sleep due to all the noise throughout the night.

reviews.title: we will definitely stay again.
reviews.positives: everything was exceptional
reviews.negatives: there wasnt anything i didnt like about the hotel

reviews.title: Beautiful hotel, friendly, nice staff.
reviews.positives: Close to venue, pretty, clean.
reviews.negatives: Only complaint- the bed pillows. They completely flatten out and I would wake up and have to try to fluff them back up. Over and over again.

reviews.title: Lovely birthday trip with our granddaughter!
reviews.positives: Best in room coffee service we’ve ever had.
